New York.name: The Ritz-Carlton New York, Central Park

Bangkok.name: Rosewood Bangkok
Bangkok.description: A contemporary luxury hotel in a 30-story tower on Ploenchit Road featuring Lakorn European Brasserie, a vertical garden, Sense spa, an infinity pool, and rooms with views over the Royal Bangkok Sports Club.

sample_reviews.title: Bangkok's newest luxury gem
sample_reviews.positive: The infinity pool views are stunning. Sense spa treatments are extraordinary. Rooms are beautifully designed with Thai touches.

Bangkok.name: Capella Bangkok
Bangkok.description: An ultra-luxury riverside hotel designed by Bill Bensley featuring a colonial-style verandah, Cote by Mauro Colagreco restaurant, private gardens, Auriga spa, and spacious suites with Chao Phraya River views.

sample_reviews.title: Thailand's finest hotel
sample_reviews.positive: Bill Bensley's design is extraordinary. Each villa suite feels like a private estate. Cote restaurant is Michelin-worthy.
sample_reviews.negative: Very expensive. Remote riverside location.

sample_reviews.title: Beyond luxury
sample_reviews.positive: The outdoor bath overlooking the river at sunrise was transcendent. Every staff member anticipates your needs.
sample_reviews.negative: Need the hotel boat shuttle to reach the BTS.

sample_reviews.title: Unparalleled hospitality
sample_reviews.positive: Capella redefines luxury in Bangkok. The gardens are lush and private. Spa is holistic and healing.

Bangkok.name: 137 Pillars Suites & Residences
Bangkok.address: 59/1 Soi Sukhumvit 39, Khlong Tan Nuea, Bangkok 10110
Bangkok.description: An all-suite luxury hotel in the heart of Sukhumvit featuring a rooftop pool and bar with panoramic city views, Jack Bain's Bar, a spa, and spacious suites with butler service.

sample_reviews.title: All-suite luxury
sample_reviews.positive: Having a full living area and kitchen makes this feel like home. Rooftop pool views are spectacular. Butler service is personal.
sample_reviews.negative: Far from historic Bangkok sights.

sample_reviews.title: Best suites in Bangkok
sample_reviews.positive: The space is incredible. Our suite was larger than most apartments. Rooftop bar is one of Bangkok's best.
sample_reviews.negative: Sukhumvit traffic can be brutal.

sample_reviews.title: Residential luxury
sample_reviews.positive: Perfect for longer stays. Kitchen and laundry in-suite. Butler pressed our clothes and arranged everything.

Bangkok.name: Hotel Muse Bangkok
Bangkok.address: 55/555 Langsuan Road, Lumpini, Pathum Wan, Bangkok 10330

sample_reviews.title: Theatrical luxury

sample_reviews.negative: Building exterior doesn't hint at the magic inside.

sample_reviews.title: Explorer's dream hotel
sample_reviews.positive: Each floor tells a different story. The attention to narrative design is unique. Cocktails at the rooftop are creative.
sample_reviews.negative: Some may find the theme overwhelming.

sample_reviews.title: Boutique perfection
sample_reviews.positive: Perfect size for personalized service. Every staff member knows your name. Design is bold and beautiful.
sample_reviews.negative: No beach proximity. Langsuan area is quiet at night.

Bangkok.name: Once Bangkok
Bangkok.description: A modern lifestyle hotel in the heart of Sukhumvit featuring a rooftop pool, co-working spaces, a farm-to-table restaurant, and contemporary rooms designed for creative travelers.

sample_reviews.title: Modern Bangkok base

sample_reviews.negative: Soi 1 can be noisy at night.

sample_reviews.title: Digital nomad friendly
sample_reviews.positive: Perfect for remote workers. Good WiFi, co-working space, and a pool to recharge. Sukhumvit is well-connected.
sample_reviews.negative: Restaurant closes early. Area nightlife may not suit everyone.

sample_reviews.title: Good value lifestyle hotel

sample_reviews.title: Old Bangkok charm
sample_reviews.positive: This hotel is like stepping back in time. The wooden shophouse architecture is beautiful. River terrace at sunset is magical.
sample_reviews.negative: Very basic amenities. No pool or gym.

sample_reviews.title: Authentic experience
sample_reviews.positive: Far from the tourist hotels, this is real Bangkok. The river views are wonderful. Chinatown food scene is incredible.
sample_reviews.negative: Difficult to find. No taxi access directly.

sample_reviews.title: Hidden riverside gem
sample_reviews.positive: The most unique hotel I've stayed at in Bangkok. Traditional Thai wooden house right on the river. Staff is warm.
sample_reviews.negative: Can be hot without strong AC. Some rooms are dark.

Bangkok.name: Praya Palazzo
Bangkok.address: 757/1 Somdej Prapinklao Road Soi 2, Bangkok Noi, Bangkok 10700
Bangkok.description: A restored 19th-century Italian-Thai palazzo on the Thonburi side of the Chao Phraya River, accessible only by private boat, featuring a riverside pool, classical architecture, and a peaceful retreat from the city.

sample_reviews.title: Hidden riverside palace
sample_reviews.positive: Arriving by private boat is unforgettable. The palazzo restoration is magnificent. Pool overlooking the river is peaceful.
sample_reviews.negative: Accessible only by boat. Must plan ahead.

sample_reviews.title: Secret Bangkok
sample_reviews.positive: This place feels like a secret. The Italian-Thai architecture is unique. River views are spectacular at dawn.
sample_reviews.negative: Isolated location. Must rely on boat shuttle for everything.

sample_reviews.title: Riverside tranquility
sample_reviews.positive: The most peaceful hotel in Bangkok. The garden and pool are lovely. Watching river traffic from the balcony is mesmerizing.
sample_reviews.negative: No restaurants within walking distance.

sample_reviews.title: Character hotel

sample_reviews.negative: Building is showing its age. Hot water can be inconsistent.
